topic: READ-ONLY meta-stats on per-cycle grade_dist sentinel emitted by tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa. Aggregate today (2026-04-27) and yesterday (2026-04-26) entries from state/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest_history.jsonl. Today: 10 cycles, 231 facts (230 [7] + 1 [10*ext]). Yesterday: 102 cycles, 1281 facts (1273 [7] + 8 [10*ext]). [7] dominates >99% on both days. No [10], [11], [11*ext] facts emitted on either day. The sentinel reports only aggregate '10*ext' bucket — it does NOT decompose into BARRIER_CONFIRMED / NEGATIVE_VERDICT / PROBE_RUN / PASS_LITERATURE subtypes, so subtype outliers are NOT visible from this history.
rule: raw 66: honest reporting — high [7] ratio is auto-classification dominance, not low rigor

core_5_op_kernel.1_intake_topic: tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa appends one JSONL line per cycle to state/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest_history.jsonl with field grade_dist='7:N,10:N,10*ext:N,11:N,11*ext:N'. 110 lines total at scan time; 102 dated 2026-04-26 and 10 dated 2026-04-27 (file is being live-appended; one entry was added between two reads).

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: history-jsonl-readable
2_pre_register_falsifiers.evidence: 110 lines parsed; each line valid JSON with expected keys

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: today-and-yesterday-filtered-correctly
2_pre_register_falsifiers.evidence: today=10 entries, yesterday=102 entries, sum=112 = file_total (live-append +2 vs initial wc=110)

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: grade-aggregation-numerically-correct
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: sum(grade_dist) per day == sum(facts) per day
2_pre_register_falsifiers.evidence: today: grade_sum=231, facts_sum=231; yesterday: grade_sum=1281, facts_sum=1281

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: outlier-detection-honest
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: outliers identified via raw counts; subtype unavailability acknowledged
2_pre_register_falsifiers.evidence: sentinel emits only aggregate '10*ext' — BARRIER/NEGATIVE/PROBE/PASS_LITERATURE subtypes NOT recoverable from history; reported limitation explicitly

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: today-vs-yesterday-comparison-evidence-based
2_pre_register_falsifiers.criterion: ratios computed from raw aggregates, not estimated

3_fanout_axes.aggregation_correctness: raw count vs facts-field cross-check; 100% match both days
3_fanout_axes.comparative_honesty: no spin — [7] dominance reflects auto-ingest classifying ω-cycle witness JSON shards as routine; [10*ext] tail requires manual literature/probe/barrier/negative tagging in source .n6

today_vs_yesterday.sample_size_warning: today=231 facts vs yesterday=1281 facts (5.5x); 1 vs 8 [10*ext] is below statistical-significance threshold
today_vs_yesterday.honest_verdict: no meaningful trend signal — both days show >99% [7] dominance with rare [10*ext] tail; today is younger session (10 cycles vs 102) and the gap will close as more cycles fire

outlier_cycles_top_3.rationale: ONLY cycle in 112-cycle window with 2 [10*ext] in single cycle (all others have 0 or 1) — likely literature-grounded or barrier-confirmed shard with multiple cited claims
today's largest single cycle by ~11x vs next-largest (16 facts); produced today's only [10*ext] entry; likely a bulk-absorption fanout shard
yesterday's largest single cycle by facts (94); ALL [7] — pure auto-classified bulk; honest evidence that big cycles do not automatically produce [10*ext]

honest_interpretation.primary_finding: >99% of all atlas facts on both days are grade [7] (auto-classified routine). [10*ext] (literature/probe/barrier/negative) is rare on both days — 8 yesterday, 1 today.
honest_interpretation.no_spin: high [7] ratio is NOT 'low rigor' — it reflects that auto-ingest of ω-cycle witness JSON predominantly classifies operational shards (kick fanout, hexa-port, smoke-bench, atlas-absorb) as routine. [10*ext] tagging requires explicit literature citations, probe runs, barrier confirmations, or negative verdicts in the source .n6 — these are deliberately rare by design.
honest_interpretation.sentinel_limitation: the per-cycle sentinel only reports the aggregate '10*ext' bucket; it does NOT decompose into [10*PASS_LITERATURE] / [10*PROBE_RUN] / [10*BARRIER_CONFIRMED] / [10*NEGATIVE_VERDICT]. To audit barrier-confirms vs negative-verdicts ratio, the n6 source files themselves would need to be grep'd for the subtype tokens.
honest_interpretation.no_trend_signal: today vs yesterday delta is -0.19pp on [10*ext] ratio — well within sampling noise (today only 10 cycles deep)

proposal.target_distribution: NO HARD TARGET. The [7]-dominant pattern is healthy for an active development session: most cycles are operational (port, smoke-bench, audit, absorb) and correctly classified routine. Forcing higher [10*ext] ratio would incentivize over-tagging — defeating the grade system's signal.
proposal.recommended_action: (a) leave aggregate ratio unmeasured-against-target; (b) optionally enhance sentinel to decompose [10*ext] into 4 subtypes ([10*PASS_LITERATURE], [10*PROBE_RUN], [10*BARRIER_CONFIRMED], [10*NEGATIVE_VERDICT]) so honesty-relevant signals (negative verdicts especially) are visible without grepping n6 source; (c) periodically re-run this kick weekly to track [10*ext] absolute count growth, not ratio.

follow_up_kicks: atlas-grade-dist-sentinel-decomposition-design — design extension of omega_cycle_atlas_ingest sentinel to emit per-subtype counts inside [10*ext] (would require change to tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa)

atlas-n6-source-subtype-grep — direct grep of n6/atlas.append.*.n6 for [10*BARRIER_CONFIRMED], [10*NEGATIVE_VERDICT], [10*PROBE_RUN], [10*PASS_LITERATURE] tokens to recover decomposition retroactively
atlas-grade-dist-weekly — schedule this analysis weekly to track [10*ext] absolute count over time
